channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Your Winter Paralympics primer: What, who and how to watch
item.description: Hundreds of para athletes are competing in Italy through March 15. Many Americans are defending past titles, with the U.S. sled hockey team hoping to ...

item.title: Oil surges to its highest price since 2023, and stocks drop after U.S. jobs report
item.description: Stocks fell Friday on worries that the economy could become stuck in a worst-case scenario of stagnating growth and high inflation. Oil prices touched...

item.title: No lawsuits required: U.S. Customs is working on a system to refund tariffs
item.description: U.S. Customs told the trade court it aims for a streamlined process in 45 days to return importers' money without requiring individual lawsuits.

item.title: Poll: A majority of Americans opposes U.S. military action in Iran
item.description: Most Americans disapprove of President Trump's handling of Iran, and a majority sees Iran as either only a minor threat or no threat at all, an NPR/ P...

item.title: Middle East conflicts largely avoided energy facilities in the past. Not in this war
item.description: Recent conflicts in the region have either spared energy infrastructure or caused limited damage. That isn't the case in the U.S.-Israeli war with Ira...

item.title: Trump demands "unconditional surrender" as Russia gives Iran intel on U.S. positions
item.description: With the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran in its seventh day, President Trump says the aim is to bring Iran back from the brink of destruction and "Make Iran ...

item.title: Beirut evacuees 'sleeping in tents and cars'
item.description: Some residents in the Beirut suburb of Dahieh have left their homes amid ongoing air strikes by Israel.

item.title: US economy unexpectedly sheds 92,000 jobs in February
item.description: The contraction came as a surprise with payrolls down in nearly every sector.

item.title: Watch Live: Rev. Jesse Jackson funeral services at House of Hope
item.description: The People's Celebration, the public funeral service for Rev. Jesse Jackson, Sr., is being held at House of Hope on the far South Side of Chicago.

item.title: Robots, already in hospitals, are ready to roll in other industries
item.description: In the years to come, robots will help offset worker shortages in health care, manufacturing and other industries, experts say.

item.title: For US Muslims, immigration crackdown fears, new war worries and anti-Muslim rhetoric cloud Ramadan
Watch: Obama honors Rev. Jesse Jackson's life and impact at his funeral
item.description: Former President Barack Obama spoke at the funeral of civil rights icon Rev. Jesse Jackson on Friday. Obama remembered Jackson as a man with "immense ...

item.title: Evidence suggests the deadly blast at an Iranian school was likely a US airstrike
'Massive' numbers killed by gunmen in latest Nigeria attack, senator tells BBC
item.description: Suspected Islamist militants reportedly seized control of a village for two days before being forced out by troops and air strikes.

item.title: These 10 jobs are most exposed to AI, Anthropic finds
item.description: The maker of the Claude chatbot says its research could help identify economic disruptions by measuring how AI is currently reshaping work.

item.title: Indonesia to ban social media and other online platforms for under 16s
item.description: It is the latest country to curb young people's access to digital spaces to try and protect them from abuse.

item.title: Ukrainian bank workers released after detention in Hungary
item.description: Kyiv has reacted furiously after officials in Budapest accused staff transporting cash to Ukraine of money laundering.

item.title: Attempted suicides, fights, pain: 911 calls reveal misery at ICEâs largest detention facility
item.description: The calls to 911 poured in from staff at Camp East Montana, the nationâs largest U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention facility, in its f...
